channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: What to know about Chris Wright, Trump's choice to run the Energy Department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Fossil fuel executive Chris Wright, Donald Trump's choice for Energy secretary, is a strong supporter of oil and gas development, including fracking, a key pillar of the president-elect’s quest for U.S. “energy dominance” in the global market.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP cries foul on Dem border spending bill they say would drag out migrant crisis
item.description: Appropriations Committee ranking member Susan Collins and other Republicans hit Democrats for advancing a "partisan" Homeland Security spending bill.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans file 12 Pennsylvania lawsuits in 'aggressive' push to end recount
item.description: The Republican Party confirmed new lawsuits to Fox News, which they say are aimed at protecting GOP Sen.-elect Dave McCormick's victory over outgoing Democrat Sen. Bob Casey.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's treasury pick could give an indication of what he plans to do about tariffs
item.description: One of the top remaining vacancies in President-elect Donald Trump’s proposed Cabinet is his treasury secretary — and the views of his top contenders there reflect broader questions about how the president-elect might wield tariffs in his new administration.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak And Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Should women be allowed to fight on the front lines? Trump's defense pick reignites debate
item.description: President Donald Trump’s nominee for defense secretary, Pete Hegseth, has reignited a debate that many thought had been long settled.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Harris campaign costs for star-studded events on election eve ballooned to over $10M: report
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris reportedly spent tens of millions of dollars on star-studded events in several swing states on the eve of Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's choice for FCC chairman says agency 'will end its promotion of DEI' next year
item.description: Brendan Carr, current senior Republican member of the FCC and President-elect Donald Trump's choice for FCC chairman, said the agency will stop promoting DEI in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's gains with minority voters fuel GOP optimism
item.description: Republicans have made gains with minority voters in the past two elections, a marked shift among some of the most loyal voting blocs for Democrats. The GOP saw an increase in support from Asian, Black, Hispanic and Latino voters in November, building on the growth the party saw from the midterms.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump taps fracking CEO Chris Wright for Energy secretary
item.description: Trump announced he would nominate Chris Wright, a donor and fracking company leader, to serve as his Energy secretary.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: UAP whistleblower says they need greater protections
item.description: Whistleblowers testified at a House of Representatives committee this week regarding the existence of UAPs or UAP-related programs.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Trump is creating team of loyalists after conflicts with Cabinet members in 1st term
item.description: In his second term, President-elect Donald Trump is surrounding himself with people who can amplify his message and agree with how he wants to remake the executive branch.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Trump names Karoline Leavitt as White House press secretary
item.description: Leavitt, 27, previously served in the Trump White House as assistant press secretary under Kayleigh McEnany, now a leading host on Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump picks Karoline Leavitt to be youngest White House press secretary in history
item.description: Leavitt will be the youngest person to ever hold the job and is expected to deliver press briefings based on Trump’s statement late Friday that he has “utmost confidence she will excel at the podium.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore, MPR News

item.title: Former Detroit Lions player charged over Capitol riot
item.description: A former Detroit Lions linebacker was arrested and charged with allegedly assaulting law enforcement and other offenses over the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol riot, according to the Department of Justice (DOJ).
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: President-elect Trump brings South Dakota politicians into national spotlight
item.description: While the next four years of President-elect Donald Trump’s administration are still being constructed, South Dakota politicians are being brought into a larger national conversation.
item.dc:creator: Phil Picardi, Heidi Raschke, and Lukas Levin, MPR News

item.title: Clark County finds 1,600 uncounted mail-in ballots in secure vault
item.description: Clark County elections officials discovered more than 1,600 uncounted mail-in ballots Thursday, more than a week after Election Day, a spokesperson said.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump spokesperson Steven Cheung to serve as White House communications director
item.description: Steven Cheung, who has served as a top press aide on the Trump campaign for the past two years, will join the incoming administration as White House communications director, President-elect Trump announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: RFK Jr. wants to 'Make America Healthy Again.' He could face a lot of pushback
item.description: RFK Jr. wants to tackle chronic disease. Despite controversial views on vaccines, his focus on healthy food and taking on special interests may find broad support — and face political headwinds.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone and Allison Aubrey, MPR News

item.title: Outspent 4-to-1, Republicans still made gains in state legislative elections
item.description: Despite outspending Republicans, Democrats didn’t achieve some ambitious goals in state legislative races. The GOP continues to control more legislatures, though Democrats notched some victories.
item.dc:creator: Ryland Barton, MPR News

item.title: Trump’s criminal defense attorneys offered DOJ roles
item.description: President-elect Trump announced Thursday he will nominate three of his personal criminal defense attorneys to serve in senior Justice Department roles. Todd Blanche will serve as deputy attorney general, and Emil Bove will serve as principal associate deputy attorney general, the department's No.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Sen. Smith: Trump cabinet picks are concerning, but I will give each a fair hearing
item.description: Minnesota U.S. Sen. Tina Smith shared she will look closely at each nomination ahead of a hearing. But Trump wants the new Senate leader to allow him to make appointments when the chamber is on recess, bypassing confirmation votes altogether.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Alanna Elder, MPR News

item.title: Satire slinger The Onion buys Alex Jones' Infowars at auction with help from Sandy Hook families
item.description: The satirical news publication The Onion won the bidding for Alex Jones’ Infowars at a bankruptcy auction, backed by families of Sandy Hook Elementary School shooting victims whom Jones owes more than $1 billion in defamation judgments for calling the massacre a hoax, the families announced Thursday
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Jacky Rosen looks forward to 2nd term with focus on economy; Nevada senator strongly opposes mass deportation
item.description: Democratic Nevada Sen. Jacky Rosen plans to focus on the economy and kitchen-table issues after voters re-elected her to a second term in Congress, she told 8 News Now.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Many Californians would back Kamala Harris for California governor, new poll suggests
item.description: It’s unclear what Vice President Kamala Harris will do after losing the presidential election, but should she run for California governor, support among state residents is seemingly strong, new polling suggests.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Matt Gaetz is Trump's pick for attorney general
item.description: President-elect Trump announced Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) will serve as his attorney general, tapping a loyal congressional ally who himself was the subject of a Justice Department investigation.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Trump draft executive order would set up board to oust generals en masse: Report
item.description: If signed by Trump once he takes office, it could allow the quick removal of generals and admirals and purge the ranks of those the future commander-in-chief takes issue with for whatever reason.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WGN 9
